title: What other mecha anime also have insert thing here
display_title: What other mecha anime also have insert thing here
wikitext:
[]: What other mecha anime also have (insert thing here)...?             Quote Post by PuppetChaos » Thu Apr 30, 2009 11:54 am Reichu gave me the idea, so I figure I'd make a "What other mecha anime/manga have (thing)...?" This thread IS NOT a "so-and-so ripped off Eva" or "Eva ripped off so-and-so" thread. Also, I use the term 'robot' very, very loosely; so Evas are robots, RahXephon is a robot, I'm a robot, etc. But I'm sure you all know what I mean. Berserker Robots: Mazinger Z - First or second chapter of the original manga, Mazinger destroys buildings while Kouji tries to stop it. Mazinkaiser - The first time Mazinkaiser is deployed, it goes nuts on the enemy, and then starts attacking its own home base. G Gundam - The Berserker system thing. While it's more the pilot going nuts, they're still no longer really in control. Ideon - The Ideon destroyed an entire planet in response to two of its pilots getting killed. Robots That Roar / Howl / Etc: Giant Robo - Typically growls after getting a command. Also cries. Shin Getter Robo - Growls/roars in Getter Robo: Armageddon, typically after combining into the Getter 1 formation. I'm pretty sure it does it elsewhere. Ideon - The Ideon howls into the night sky at the end of an episode after the main character experiences the death of a loved one. You could also argue that some of the noises it makes are 'vocalizations' of a sort. Mazinger - I can swear Mazinger roars/growls. Can't remember exactly. Robots That Refuse Control / Do What They Want: Turn A Gundam - Attacks the Moonrace forces early on by itself, later on it automatically seeks out an ancient weapons cache. The Moonlight Butterfly system (machines that eat technology/energy) seems to activate at the Turn A's discretion, but in response to the pilot. I may be wrong on that. RahXephon - It automatically seeks out Ayato whenever an enemy is coming, it sometimes does things by itself in a battle. Big O - It locks up on several occasions. Once is to keep one of its attacks from killing R. Dorothy. Another time it hesitates because it's "scared" --- Feel free to add more, guys!

[]: Re: What other mecha anime also have (insert thing here)...?             Quote Post by Alaska Slim » Thu Apr 30, 2009 12:40 pm PuppetChaos wrote: I'm a robot,

[]:             Quote Post by SaltyJoe » Thu Apr 30, 2009 1:05 pm If we can depart from the mechas themselves, i would say: "Expression Impaired Pilot (a la Rei):" Setsuna F. Seiei from Gundam-00. I only watched the first six episodes of Season 01, but by my first impression the kid makes Rei look positively passionate.

[]:             Quote Post by PuppetChaos » Thu Apr 30, 2009 1:18 pm That Toonami promo is awesome. SaltyJoe wrote: If we can depart from the mechas themselves, i would say:

[]:             Quote Post by Alaska Slim » Fri May 01, 2009 8:16 am SaltyJoe wrote: Setsuna F. Seiei from Gundam-00. I only watched the first six episodes of Season 01, but by my first impression the kid makes Rei look positively passionate.

[]:             Quote Post by Guyver Spawn » Sat May 02, 2009 12:39 pm Not a anime mecha but Kiryu goes nuts and destroying stuff once it hears Godzilla roar since it has the soul of the original Godzilla that attack tokyo in 1954.

[]:             Quote Post by PuppetChaos » Sat May 02, 2009 11:52 pm I just re-downloaded Ideon and watched the episode I cited, and it was really the Ideon amplifying the pilot's scream.... or maybe screaming in unison. Hard to tell. I am totally going to have to do that "Ideon and Eva" comparison thing I wanna do. Guyver Spawn wrote: Not a anime mecha but Kiryu goes nuts and destroying stuff once it hears Godzilla roar since it has the soul of the original Godzilla that attack tokyo in 1954.

[]:             Quote Post by Guyver Spawn » Sat May 02, 2009 11:57 pm That from that new(ish) Godzilla movie?

[]:             Quote Post by Evayo » Sun May 03, 2009 1:17 am Holy crap, deja vu! As soon as that guy said "Mr. Norman..." I remembered every word of that commercial to the letter, and I haven't even seen it in like nine years. ~:Evayo:~

[]:             Quote Post by honsou » Sun May 03, 2009 11:58 am Ideon at the very end at times becomes completely uncontrollable. Such as when Ideon saves some sort of slug animal and teleporting some of the Solo crew onto the head buff clan ship with the ability to speak their language(Yeah Ideon is basically the god bot).

[]:             Quote Post by Dr. Nick » Sun May 03, 2009 4:12 pm Berserk robots are real, son. PuppetChaos wrote: He gets more emotional as the series goes on (second season more noticeably), but yeah, kid's pretty most of the time. (but I would be too if I were raised by terrorists)

[]:             Quote Post by honsou » Sun May 03, 2009 6:03 pm The only thing I'd add is that at about episode 30, the crew finally figures out that Ideon is a sentient being that wont act upon their commands. Their reactions is almost completely copied in the Eva episode where unit 01 goes berserker. If you want an exact episode number I could get it for you.

[]:             Quote Post by PuppetChaos » Sun May 03, 2009 9:40 pm Dr. Nick wrote: Berserk robots are real, son.

[]: Re: What other mecha anime also have (insert thing here)...?             Quote Post by Joseph the PRPD » Thu May 07, 2009 12:17 pm PuppetChaos wrote: Berserker Robots: Mazinger Z - First or second chapter of the original manga, Mazinger destroys buildings while Kouji tries to stop it. Mazinkaiser - The first time Mazinkaiser is deployed, it goes nuts on the enemy, and then starts attacking its own home base. G Gundam - The Berserker system thing. While it's more the pilot going nuts, they're still no longer really in control. Ideon - The Ideon destroyed an entire planet in response to two of its pilots getting killed.

[]:             Quote Post by Dr. Nick » Tue May 19, 2009 2:49 pm PuppetChaos, are you familiar with Mobile Suit Gundam: The Blue Destiny ? The titular mechs are pretty much the closest things to "Gundam Evas" ever (although the EXAM system seems to be explained in a rather simplified way on that MAHQ page). Naturally, the BD unit 1 can go berserk, complete with red glowing eyes, making it the most brutal GM ever.

[]:             Quote Post by PuppetChaos » Tue May 19, 2009 3:45 pm Dr. Nick wrote: PuppetChaos, are you familiar with Mobile Suit Gundam: The Blue Destiny ?

[]:             Quote Post by Dr. Nick » Sat Jun 13, 2009 4:27 pm PuppetChaos wrote: I also could've sworn some sidestory in UC Gundam had Gundams that had AIs that automatically took control if it was a situation where survival was beyond the abilities of a human pilot. The ALICE system or something.

[]:             Quote Post by BattleMonkey » Sat Jun 13, 2009 8:03 pm There was also similar AI systems in F90, the in the later crossbone series, Sentinel, and Blue Destiny. Also G-Saviour PS2 game story line.
